source_pr.title: distsql: clean the memory usage of MemTracker when a query ends (#10893)
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

closes #10893

### What is changed and how it works?

Correctly release memory consuming in distsql.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test
 - Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

run a select query like `select * from t, ta`

```mysql
mysql> SELECT * FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.PROCESSLIST;
+------+------+-----------+------+---------+------+-------+----------------------------------------------+------+
| ID   | USER | HOST      | DB   | COMMAND | TIME | STATE | INFO                                         | MEM  |
+------+------+-----------+------+---------+------+-------+----------------------------------------------+------+
|    1 | root | 127.0.0.1 | test | Sleep   |    3 | 2     |                                              |    0 |
|    2 | root | 127.0.0.1 | test | Query   |    0 | 2     | SELECT * FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.PROCESSLIST |    0 |
+------+------+-----------+------+---------+------+-------+----------------------------------------------+------+
2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

Code changes

 - None

Side effects

 - Increased code complexity

Related changes

 - None

root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR fixes deterministic memory accounting/cleanup for distsql MemTracker when a query ends, and updates related tests. The case evidence ("ddl: fix data race") is mismatched with the PR change and does not provide a concrete flaky trigger, so keep the conservative fallback classification.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).
